name: M.S. in Artificial Intelligence

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The 24 referenced courses cover a strong, current AI core with a sensible vertical path (DS 675 Machine Learning -> DS 677 Deep Learning -> DS 680 Natural Language Processing at depth 2), plus CS 670 Artificial Intelligence, DS 669 Reinforcement Learning, CS 681 Computer Vision, CS 732 Advanced Machine Learning, and a DS 700B/DS 701B project-or-thesis capstone. Coherence is limited by structure, however: everything sits in one undifferentiated 'Courses (24 credits) + MS Thesis' pool (requirementGroups=1) with no separation of a required core from electives, so progression is implied rather than enforced.
dimensions.suggestion: Split the single pool into a required foundational core (e.g., DS 675, DS 677, DS 637) and themed elective tracks (vision, NLP, trustworthy/HPC) so the intended progression is explicit.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: The prerequisite graph is healthy: missingCourseCount=0 (every referenced code resolves) and deepestPrereqChain=3 gives real vertical depth (e.g., DS 675 -> DS 677 -> DS 680; MGMT 735 sits at depth 3), which is appropriate for a 30-credit M.S. without over-constraining entry. Gates are reasonable, with foundational courses like DS 675 and DS 637 kept at depth 0 for accessibility.
dimensions.suggestion: Convert prose gates such as DS 644's 'Permission of the instructor' into explicit course or competency prerequisites so the clean graph is also transparent to applicants.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or educational objectives appear anywhere in the provided data; the page conveys only a course pool and a credit label, which is a genuine gap for an M.S. catalog page.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish 4-6 measurable AI program outcomes (e.g., design and evaluate deep models, reason about model trustworthiness/fairness, deploy ML on large data) mapped to DS 677, DS 789, and CS 735.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: The stated total is 30 credits, but the deterministic reconcile shows summedTermCredits=39 against statedTotalCredits=30 (creditReconcileDelta=9.0), and the sole requirement group is labeled 'Courses (24 credits) + MS Thesis' yet carries a parsed credits value of 6.0. The 24+6 thesis intent is defensible, but the numbers as published do not reconcile.
dimensions.suggestion: Reconcile the plan so the elective pool (24 cr), thesis/project (6 cr), and the 30-credit total add up explicitly, removing the 9-credit discrepancy.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: Course entries are complete (titles and 3-credit values present) and descriptions are substantive, but the plan is presented as one flat bullet group whose name still carries markup artifacts ('* Courses (24 credits) + MS Thesis') and footnote markers (e.g., DS 637 title ends in ' *'), and there is no program overview or labeled core/elective structure.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a structured plan-of-study table with labeled core/elective groups and resolve the stray bullet/asterisk artifacts in group and course titles.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The curriculum is highly current for AI practice: DS 677 Deep Learning, DS 680 Natural Language Processing, DS 669 Reinforcement Learning, DS 789 Trustworthy Artificial Intelligence, CS 681 Computer Vision, and CS 735 High Performance Data Analytics all reflect contemporary methods. The notable gap for a 2026 AI M.S. is the absence of a dedicated large-language-model / generative-AI course and an explicit MLOps/deployment offering; NLP (DS 680) touches modern language models but does not stand in for foundation-model engineering.
dimensions.suggestion: Add an explicit LLM / generative-AI course (foundation models, fine-tuning, retrieval-augmented generation) and an MLOps/deployment elective to match current industry workflows.

descriptionQuality.justification: Descriptions are present, specific, and non-placeholder across the sample (e.g., DS 675 details Bayesian methods and hands-on real-data practice; DS 789 explains why trustworthiness matters for ML deployment; CS 735 describes scalable data-science algorithm design), with every sampled course carrying a 3-credit value.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites are mostly explicit and consistent with no dangling references (missingCourseCount=0), and disjunctive gates are well formed (DS 677 'DS 675 or CS 675 or approval of the instructor'; CS 732 'CS 634 or CS 670'), but a few rely on prose ('Permission of the instructor' for DS 644; 'As determined by nature of topic area' for DS 786).

missingTopics.rationale: With DS 680 NLP present but no dedicated foundation-model course, the program lacks explicit coverage of transformer pretraining, fine-tuning, and RAG that now define applied AI.
CS 735 covers scalable analytics but no course addresses productionizing models (CI/CD, serving, monitoring, drift), a core industry competency.
DS 789 Trustworthy AI is a strong anchor but could be extended with a course on alignment, red-teaming, and emerging AI regulation/governance.
Deep learning (DS 677) and pattern recognition (CS 782) assume optimization background, yet no dedicated convex/stochastic-optimization course appears in the pool.

emergingTrends.rationale: CS 681 Computer Vision and DS 680 NLP exist as silos; a multimodal course would connect them to vision-language models driving current research.
Modernizing DS 680/CS 670 with RAG and tool-using agent architectures would align the degree with how LLM applications are now built.
Given CS 735 High Performance Data Analytics, a module on quantization, distillation, and efficient inference would position graduates for edge and cost-constrained deployment.
DS 789 can be expanded to cover EU-AI-Act-style compliance, auditing, and fairness tooling increasingly required of AI teams.

topSuggestions: Add a dedicated LLM/generative-AI course and an MLOps/deployment elective to close the biggest currency gaps.
Reconcile the credit plan so the 24-credit coursework, thesis/project, and 30-credit total add up (eliminate the 9-credit delta).
Restructure the single course pool into a labeled required core plus themed elective tracks.
Publish measurable program-level learning outcomes mapped to key courses (DS 677, DS 789, CS 735).
Clean up presentation artifacts (bullet/asterisk markers) and replace prose prerequisite gates with explicit conditions.
